summary:
Free radical damage contributes to increased rates of fetal anomalies in diabetics; around 6-10% of affected pregnancies result in major birth defects while up to 1-2% have characteristic features like a single umbilical artery. Insulin treatment during pregnancy doesn’t guarantee prevention or correction of these conditions due to variable outcomes based on individual cases' severity and other factors involved.
The summary effectively captures the essential aspects regarding risk factors associated with gestational diabetes leading to congenital malformations without mentioning unnecessary details such as the exact percentage figures which can vary slightly across studies. It also correctly highlights that administering insulin cannot be considered definitive protection against complications given its context-dependent nature rather than being an absolute preventive agent1 (Round 1 summarized): Free radical damage contributes to increased rates of fetal anomalies in diabetics; around 6-10% of affected pregnancies result in major birth defects while up to 1-2% have characteristic features like a single umbilical artery. Insulin treatment during pregnancy doesn’t guarantee prevention or correction of these conditions due to variable outcomes based on individual cases' severity and other factors involved.
The summary effectively captures the essential aspects regarding risk factors associated with gestational diabetes leading to congenital malformations without mentioning unnecessary details such as the exact percentage figures which can vary slightly across studies. It also correctly highlights that administering insulin cannot be considered definitive protection against complications given its context-dependent nature rather than being an absolute preventive
